summary: PR #2498 improved matching in src/melee/lb/lbmemory by adding structured allocator/LBMgr/MemEntry fields, decompiling relocation and init routines, replacing some raw allocator offsets with typed fields, adding string/data references used by matched code, and documenting Handle.x8_hi behavior. It was closed without a recorded merge. Bot report showed 4 new matches and one improved unmatched function, while the PR body stated two functions remained imperfect due to saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling.

key_files.role: Main implementation changed: allocator structs, named lbMemory_804318B0 storage, string references, lbMemory_80015320 relocation routine, lbMemory_8001564C init routine, typed x6A0_mgr access, AR/devcom includes, and scoped dont_inline pragma.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: decomp-dev bot report for GALE01

key_files.role: Header comment update for Handle.x8_hi clarifying dual use as arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles.

what_changed.text: Added struct MemEntry and struct LBMgr, and replaced several raw byte ranges in struct Allocator with typed fields: x8_mem[0x83], x638_heap[6], and x6A0_mgr.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct MemEntry, struct LBMgr, struct Allocator changes

what_changed.text: Changed allocator storage from a static g_alloc object to global lbMemory_804318B0 plus a g_alloc macro alias, with static asserts for MemEntry and allocator size.
Added named string/data references for lbmemory assert/report text, including lbmemory.c, _p(free_heap), _p(free_mem), memp_kouho, report text, handle, 0, and p->size.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_803BA2C0 through lbl_803BA368 and lbMemory_804D3788/3790/3794

what_changed.text: Decompiled lbMemory_80015320 as a relocation/callback routine using HSD_DevComRequest for low addresses and an LBMgr alarm path for other transfers.
Decompiled lbMemory_8001564C init routine, including ARAlloc/ARFree/ARGetSize setup, free-list initialization loop for x8_mem, heap free-list setup, and initial lbMemory_80014E24 call.
Matched fn_80015184 by using typed x6A0_mgr access and OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) with fn_80015184 callback in OSSetAlarm.
Removed speculative explanatory comments in lbmemory.c and replaced one Handle.x8_hi uncertainty comment in lbmemory.h with a behavior-specific comment.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: cleans speculative comments while keeping verified Handle field behavior

smart_moves.text: The PR converted allocator byte padding into typed local structs where offsets were known, making later code use &g_alloc.x6A0_mgr instead of pointer arithmetic at +0x6A0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct Allocator x6A0_mgr and fn_80015184 temp_r3 = &g_alloc.x6A0_mgr

smart_moves.text: The init routine used a natural loop to link 0x83 MemEntry slots rather than spelling every x8_mem link manually.
The PR separated proven Handle.x8_hi behavior from uncertainty by updating the header comment to describe both observed roles.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: keeping only verified Handle field behavior

decomp_lessons.lesson: For allocator-like TUs, replacing anonymous byte ranges with narrow temporary structs can unlock typed access and improve matching while preserving address-style field names when semantics remain incomplete.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: new matches and improvements in main/melee/lb/lbmemory

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a field has different observed roles in different list contexts, document the contextual behavior rather than forcing a single over-specific name.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: x8_hi comment changed to arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles
human_pr_text: keeping only verified Handle field behavior

decomp_lessons.lesson: Remaining score gaps can be explicitly attributed to codegen issues such as saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling when control-flow and size shape already match.

naming_conventions.name: Allocator fields
naming_conventions.convention: Used offset-prefixed names such as x0_arenaLo, x4_arenaHi, x8_mem, x62C_free_mem, x638_heap, and x6A0_mgr, combining offsets with conservative role names.

naming_conventions.name: Temporary recovered structs
naming_conventions.convention: Used simple domain names MemEntry and LBMgr while retaining offset-prefixed member names inside them.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct MemEntry and struct LBMgr

naming_conventions.name: Handle.x8_hi comment
naming_conventions.convention: Kept existing address-style field name x8_hi but clarified known contextual meaning in a comment rather than renaming the field.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced typed overlay structs for allocator subregions to replace some raw pointer arithmetic and improve code shape.
Used OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) rather than a raw tick constant in OSSetAlarm where it matched fn_80015184 and lbMemory_80015320 alarm setup.
Used a scoped #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around lbMemory_8001564C.
Used explicit named string/data symbols for assert/report text to match .data and .sdata items.
PR body reported verification with python configure.py && ninja and pre-commit checks including match regression checks.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code +924 bytes, matched data +216 bytes, 4 new matches, and lbMemory_80015320 improved to 96.11%.
No human review comments or review records are present in the supplied PR slice.

follow_up_queries: lbmemory typed allocator fields x8_mem x638_heap x6A0_mgr
lbMemory_80015320 saved register allocation instruction scheduling
lbMemory_8001564C dont_inline pragma init free list
Handle x8_hi arena high bound allocation size child handles
lbmemory assert string data lbl_803BA2C0 lbMemory_804D3788

Use of named string/data symbols, a #define alias for g_alloc, and a dont_inline pragma are recorded as historical tactics, not promoted as general standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed allocator subregions can improve lbmemory matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2498, lbmemory matching improved after anonymous allocator byte ranges were replaced with local structs and arrays such as MemEntry x8_mem[0x83], Handle x638_heap[6], and LBMgr x6A0_mgr. This supports a reusable lesson to prefer narrow recovered types over raw allocator padding when offsets and use sites support them.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: lbMemory_8001564C and fn_80015184 reached 100.00%; lbMemory_80015320 improved to 96.11%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Document contextual field roles without over-renaming
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2498 changed Handle.x8_hi's comment from an uncertainty note to a contextual behavior note: arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles. This is a useful conservative naming/documentation pattern when one address-style field has multiple proven roles.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: keeping only verified Handle field behavior

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/lb/lbmemory belongs to library lbmemory allocator work
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 changed src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c and src/melee/lb/lbmemory.h for lbmemory allocator matching, including allocator structs, Handle behavior, relocation, AR/devcom transfer setup, and initialization.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for curator review; do not infer broader ownership beyond this PR slice.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma exceptions should remain tightly evidenced
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 used a scoped #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop block around lbMemory_8001564C, which the bot reported as a new 100.00% match. This supports, but does not expand, the existing standard that pragma exceptions need tight scope and evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Route as a standards reinforcement candidate only; current loaded standards already cover avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics-need-evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Data/string symbol changes need explicit ownership evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 introduced named string/data symbols for lbmemory assert/report text and bot output reported .data and .sdata matches. Because current standards warn against routine literal-to-symbol promotion, this PR should only be used as a curator-reviewed example of data-backed matching when section evidence is available, not as a blanket endorsement.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential curated exception/example; avoid promoting string-symbol replacement as normal code-matching style.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the #define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0 pattern as a naming convention or accepted global aliasing tactic; the PR evidence only shows it was used here.
Do not claim lbMemory_80014FC8 or lbMemory_80015320 fully matched; the PR body says they remained at 99.324326% and 96.106800%.
Do not infer reviewer approval, merge acceptance, or requested changes; no human review comments are in the slice and merged_at is null.
Do not generalize named assert/report string globals as a preferred style; current standards require data ownership evidence and caution around string literal symbol regressions.
